spring-like day March, CPT's founding director Gene Stoltzfus completed his journey earth the age 70. His heart stopped while was riding his bicycle near his home Fort Frances, Ontario. survived his wife Dorothy Friesen and many, many peacemakers who stand the broad shoulders his lifetime creative action.
Gene nurtured and shaped Christian Peacemaker Teams from its beginning almost years ago. director for the first years CPT's organizational life, Gene's vision, ideas, and initiatives laid strong foundation for our present work. His style, faith, humor, strengths, shortcomings and quirks are embedded CPT. His hand remains evident CPT's training and deployment long-term, diverse, disciplined and empowered teams; team and office meals and shared check-ins; CPT's creative direct action reduce violence and highlight injustice; patterns prayer and mutual support sending each other out truly blessed; CPT's attentiveness exposing privilege and undoing oppression; and CPT's deep roots connection throughout the Church, which turn transformed into hotbed peacemaking.
Gene brought tremendous energy, courage and commitment the work Christian peacemaking. During his tenure director, CPT grew from dream and dialog about active peacemaking, sustaining and supporting full-time field teams with hundreds trained CPTers working partnership with local peacemakers conflict zones across the globe.
Gene saw military culture worldwide and asked how the church could create counter-culture. held out vision world free from war and successfully passed that vision new generation CPTers. saw us, and helped become, competent, capable peacemakers. imagine him now, already organizing the Celestial Peacemaker Teams.
Thank-you, Gene. miss you.

Retailers know what's happening out there. It's tough environment trying get ahead.
More and more are seeing value being part the local neighbourhood trading scene.
"Connect with your local suppliers and celebrate local produce.
Let everyone know that you're using local supplies and/ local services."
"Everyone loves the "home grown" idea.
Try offer lower prices for local products and services.
Promote "fresh" you can too."
"It's great feeling being local trader"because you know that more your money will kept your local economy.
The more money spent, the more the area prospers. It's energising thought."
"You create local jobs for local people when you're local trader.
Hiring from the local community means you immediately have connection with those around the area."
"Small local businesses can become involved community events and result, customers support us.
give more personalized service and the good thing for then that the rewards are there."
"Buying from locally owned business helps the environment.
conserves energy and resources, for instance, less fuel for transportation and less packaging waste.
So, embrace your local community. Get know what makes tick and support it!
